blocks.html:
Ch 1 Introduction & Architecture 45 min ▶
IntroWhat is Bitstream Studio? Workshop goals, tools we'll use, expected outcomes
TheoryIoT data pipeline — sensor → firmware → bridge → provider → UI. Event-driven architecture. WebSocket transport. Bitstream event envelope (type, v, payload). R0 read vs R1 command tiers
DemoOpen Bitstream Studio, connect simulator, observe live bitstream:sample events in browser DevTools. Identify sensor IDs and field keys in real payloads
Use CasesIndustry 4.0: condition monitoring, predictive maintenance, remote dashboards. When to use simulator vs live hardware
SummaryArchitecture diagram recap. Key vocab: catalog, sample, config, stale. Setup checklist for remaining chapters
Ch 2 Sensor Telemetry — Reading & Visualizing Live Data 75 min ▶
IntroWhat does "live telemetry" mean in industrial IoT? From sensor sample to dashboard in milliseconds
Theorybitstream:catalog structure — field keys, units, min/max, maskChannels. bitstream:sample payload anatomy. Range mapping formula: pct = clamp((v−min)/(max−min)×100, 0, 100). Mock fallback pattern. Status labeling (live / mock / stale)
DemoBuild a live SHT40 temperature + humidity dual progress bar. Connect to WS provider; implement mock fallback. Display field value, unit, and percent. Observe bitstream:stale event
Use CasesHVAC monitoring, cold-chain logistics, cleanroom humidity control. Guidelines: always label data source, always implement stale detection, never hardcode sensor range
SummaryCatalog-driven range mapping. WS + postMessage dual transport. Mock fallback is mandatory, not optional. Review trainee progress bars
Ch 3 Sensor Configuration 60 min ▶
IntroWhy does sensor configuration matter? Sampling rate, publish mode, and power trade-offs
Theorybitstream:config payload — enabled, publishMode (periodic / on_change / hybrid), mask, samplingIntervalMs, deltaX100. R1 commands: sensor.cfg.set, bmi270.mode.set. Command/response flow with requestId. Config limits from catalog sharedConfigLimits
DemoUse Sensor Telemetry panel to change BMI270 sampling rate. Observe config event update in UI. Send bmi270.mode.set raw + sensor.cfg.set mask=2 to enable gyro axis. Verify gyroX appears in subsequent samples
Use CasesBattery-powered edge devices — on_change mode for slow drift sensors. High-frequency capture for vibration analysis. Rate/mask optimization for bandwidth-limited links
SummaryConfig → command → response → new config cycle. publishMode selection guide. Common mistakes: polling config instead of event-driven, ignoring maskLabels
Ch 4 Sensor Studio — Node Editor Basics 75 min ▶
IntroVisual programming for sensor data — why node editors suit IoT signal processing
TheoryNode types: Sensor nodes (source), Math nodes (transform), Visualization nodes (sink). Data flow graph — directed acyclic. Port types: numeric scalar, vector, boolean. Execution model: event-driven push on each sample
DemoCreate a flow: SHT40 sensor node → gauge visualization node. Add a Math node to convert °C → °F. Connect DPS368 pressure node → numeric display. Run graph, observe live values updating
Use CasesRapid prototyping without code, educational environments, field technician dashboards. When to graduate from Sensor Studio to custom HTML
SummaryNode categories recap. Port connection rules. Saving and sharing flows. Keyboard shortcuts. Common errors: disconnected ports, type mismatches
Ch 5 Building Pipelines — Math, Logic & Multi-Sensor Flows 75 min ▶
IntroFrom single-sensor readout to multi-sensor processing pipelines — the core of Industry 4.0 analytics
TheoryMath nodes: arithmetic, scale/offset, moving average, threshold comparator. Logic nodes: AND/OR, latch, timer. Multi-sensor sync: combining async streams. Vector magnitude: |a| = √(x²+y²+z²) for IMU / mag. Signal conditioning concepts: filtering, deadband
DemoBuild: BMI270 accel → magnitude node → threshold → alert indicator. Build: BMM350 magX/Y/Z → 3-axis compass rose visualization. Combine SHT40 temp + DPS368 pressure into a weather station display panel
Use CasesVibration fault detection, orientation monitoring for robotics, environmental station for agriculture IoT, fall detection (accel magnitude spike)
SummaryPipeline design principles: one concern per node, name your nodes, test incrementally. Performance: avoid redundant math nodes on high-rate streams
Ch 6 Custom HTML Dashboard — All 4 Sensors 75 min ▶
IntroWhen to build a custom HTML dashboard vs Sensor Studio. Deploying to Course Studio iframes
TheoryDual transport: WS standalone vs postMessage iframe. bitstream:ready handshake. Catalog-driven range init. All 4 sensors: BMI270 (gyro/accel/euler), SHT40 (temp/humidity), DPS368 (pressure/temp), BMM350 (magX/Y/Z) — teal vs violet color coding. Mock fallback architecture. Stale detection per-sensor
DemoBuild complete 4-sensor dashboard: BMI270 6-axis bars + pitch/roll gauges · SHT40 temp+humidity cards · DPS368 sea-level pressure ring · BMM350 3-axis magnetic field (violet). Live WS with mock fallback. Connection status badge. Sensor stale indicator
Use CasesIndustrial HMI panels, remote monitoring dashboards, museum/exhibit interactives, embedded webview dashboards on edge devices
SummaryFull workshop recap. Design patterns checklist. Extending to new sensors. Publishing and embedding. Trainee project ideas
Total ~7.5 hrs
6 chapters · 30 sections
